title: Just as, Venerable Sir, a person carries a bowl full of oil, with many holes, many openings, leaking, dripping. So too, Venerable Sir, I carry…
content: Just as, Venerable Sir, a person carries a bowl full of oil, with many holes, many openings, leaking, dripping. So too, Venerable Sir, I carry this body, with many holes, many openings, leaking, dripping. Venerable Sir, for one who does not have mindfulness of the body established, if he offends a fellow celibate, he might go off wandering without apologizing.

title: He possesses the virtue loved by the noble ones, unbroken, unblemished, untarnished, continuously practiced, without flaw, leading to freedom, praised by the wise, not defiled,…
content: He possesses the virtue loved by the noble ones, unbroken, unblemished, untarnished, continuously practiced, without flaw, leading to freedom, praised by the wise, not defiled, conducive to concentration.

title: Furthermore, Venerable Sir, I see bhikkhus joyful, elated, rapturous, with serene faculties, not agitated, calm, living on the alms of others, with minds like wild…
content: Furthermore, Venerable Sir, I see bhikkhus joyful, elated, rapturous, with serene faculties, not agitated, calm, living on the alms of others, with minds like wild deer. Venerable Sir, I think: 'Surely these venerable ones have realized some supreme, excellent distinction in the Blessed One's teaching, a progressive attainment, that they are so joyful, elated, rapturous, with serene faculties, not agitated, calm, living on the alms of others, with minds like wild deer.' Venerable Sir, this is my conviction regarding the Blessed One: 'The Blessed One is perfectly enlightened; the Dhamma is well taught by the Blessed One; the Saṅgha of the Blessed One's disciples is well practiced and well trained.'

title: I do not see any other single thing, bhikkhus, that so prevents the arising of unarisen sloth and torpor, or leads to the abandoning of…
content: I do not see any other single thing, bhikkhus, that so prevents the arising of unarisen sloth and torpor, or leads to the abandoning of arisen sloth and torpor, as the element of energy, the element of exertion, the element of striving. For one who is energetic and striving, bhikkhus, unarisen sloth and torpor do not arise, and arisen sloth and torpor are abandoned.

title: And, bhikkhus, what is the cessation of suffering? Dependent on the eye and forms, eye-consciousness arises. The meeting of the three is contact. With contact…
content: And, bhikkhus, what is the cessation of suffering? Dependent on the eye and forms, eye-consciousness arises. The meeting of the three is contact. With contact as condition, feeling arises. With feeling as condition, craving arises. With the complete fading away and cessation of that craving, clinging ceases. With the cessation of clinging, existence ceases. With the cessation of existence, birth ceases. With the cessation of birth, aging, death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, grief, and despair cease. Thus is the cessation of this whole mass of suffering. This is the cessation of suffering. Dependent on the ear ... on the nose ... on the tongue ... on the body ... Dependent on the mind and mind-objects, mind-consciousness arises. The meeting of the three is contact. With contact as condition, feeling arises. With feeling as condition, craving arises. With the complete fading away and cessation of that craving, clinging ceases ... Thus is the cessation of this whole mass of suffering. This is the cessation of suffering.

title: Ānanda, if any of you thinks: 'The Teacher's word is no more, we have no teacher,' Ānanda, do not think such a thought. Ānanda, the…
content: Ānanda, if any of you thinks: 'The Teacher's word is no more, we have no teacher,' Ānanda, do not think such a thought. Ānanda, the Dhamma and Discipline that I have taught and explained, after my passing, that Dhamma and Discipline will be your teacher.

title: Elder Ajjuna: I can draw water from the dry land, Like a great flood sweeping away, I enter the truth.
content:
Elder Ajjuna:

I can draw water from the dry land,
Like a great flood sweeping away, I enter the truth.

title: Bhikkhus, sensual pleasures are impermanent, empty, false, of a delusive nature. Bhikkhus, this is the empty chatter of a fool built on falsehood. Present sensual…
content: Bhikkhus, sensual pleasures are impermanent, empty, false, of a delusive nature. Bhikkhus, this is the empty chatter of a fool built on falsehood. Present sensual pleasures and future sensual pleasures, present perceptions of sensual pleasure and future perceptions of sensual pleasure, both belong to Māra's realm, are Māra's domain, Māra's bait, Māra's feeding ground.

title: He avoids wrong livelihood such as: using magical rites for peace, purification rituals, bathing rituals, sacrifice rituals, making oneself vomit, purging, expelling impurities ... treating…
content: He avoids wrong livelihood such as: using magical rites for peace, purification rituals, bathing rituals, sacrifice rituals, making oneself vomit, purging, expelling impurities ... treating eye diseases, practicing medicine, surgery on children, administering root medicines, neutralizing the effects of medicines.

title: Elder Somamitta: Like climbing a small plank on the ocean, one sinks; So too, one of good conduct sinks when associating with others. Therefore avoid…
content:
Elder Somamitta:

Like climbing a small plank on the ocean, one sinks;
So too, one of good conduct sinks when associating with others.
Therefore avoid the lazy, the indolent.

Live near the noble ones, the secluded, the energetic,
Meditative, always striving, the wise and clear-sighted.
